prompt: Fix a misleading runbook comment in hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix (the valley backup enablement runbook, next to services.valley.backup). The example restic repository URL in the comment currently reads 'sftp://u123456@u123456.your-storagebox.de:23//./backups/valley' — this absolute-at-root form FAILS on Hetzner Storage Boxes: the sftp account lands in /home and the box root is read-only, so restic init dies with a generic 'sftp Failure (SSH_FX_FAILURE)' on MkdirAll (this happened for real today). Correct the example to the form that works: 'sftp://u123456@u123456.your-storagebox.de:23//home/backups/valley' and add one short clause noting the account lands in /home and the box root is not writable. Touch only that comment block (and the same example if it appears in docs/valley-git-hosting.md — grep for 'your-storagebox' to catch all occurrences). Verify nix flake check passes (comment-only change). Tiny PR; body = two sentences on the real failure.
